highlights.text: 10 sources — NixOS, Home Manager, nix-darwin, Flakes, FlakeHub, NixVim, wiki, nix.dev, Noogle, NixHub
Package search — find packages, programs, and options across channels

Flake inputs — browse and read flake input metadata from FlakeHub
Docs search — wiki, nix.dev guides, and Noogle function reference
No auth required — all queries hit public APIs

readme:
### What it does

NixOS MCP gives your AI agent comprehensive reference access to the NixOS ecosystem. Two tools cover package search, option lookup, version history, flake discovery, and documentation search across 10 sources.

Your agent can find the right package name, check available NixOS or Home Manager options, look up function signatures via Noogle, trace version history across Nixpkgs channels, and browse flake inputs — all without leaving the conversation.

### Tool coverage

| Tool | What it does |
|------|--------------|
| `nix` | Search, info, stats, options, channels, flake-inputs, and cache queries across NixOS, Home Manager, nix-darwin, Flakes, FlakeHub, NixVim, wiki, nix.dev, Noogle, and NixHub |
| `nix_versions` | Package version history from NixHub.io — find which Nixpkgs commit introduced a specific version |

### Source coverage

| Source | What it searches |
|--------|------------------|
| `nixos` | NixOS packages and options (search.nixos.org) |
| `home-manager` | Home Manager options and programs |
| `darwin` | nix-darwin options (macOS system configuration) |
| `flakes` | Flake metadata and inputs |
| `flakehub` | FlakeHub registry — versioned flake distribution |
| `nixvim` | NixVim plugin options |
| `wiki` | NixOS Wiki articles |
| `nix-dev` | nix.dev guides and tutorials |
| `noogle` | Nix function reference (type signatures, docs) |
| `nixhub` | NixHub.io package version history |

### No auth required

All queries hit public APIs — no API keys, tokens, or accounts needed. Install and use immediately.

### Use cases

- **NixOS configuration authoring** — find package names, check option types and defaults
- **Declarative infrastructure** — pair with VM provisioning MCPs to build NixOS-based infrastructure
- **Home Manager / nix-darwin setup** — search options for dotfile and macOS management
- **Flake development** — discover flake inputs, check FlakeHub registry
- **Debugging** — trace package version history to find regressions

setup.blurb: NixOS ecosystem search — packages, options, flakes, and version history. No credentials needed.
setup.help_text: No configuration required. All queries use public NixOS APIs. Install and use immediately.

scenarios.label: Provision a NixOS VM on Vultr
scenarios.body: Search for NixOS packages needed for the server role, look up configuration options, then use vultr-blade-mcp to provision a VM with cloud-init that bootstraps NixOS with the discovered configuration.

scenarios.label: Configure a NixOS service with DNS
scenarios.body: Look up NixOS service options (e.g. services.nginx, services.ntfy-sh), check the package version history for stability, then configure DNS records via Cloudflare for the new service endpoint.

scenarios.label: Audit Nixpkgs version availability
scenarios.body: Check version history for critical packages across channels, identify which Nixpkgs commit provides the required versions, and verify option compatibility between NixOS and Home Manager.

certification.security_notes: Read-only. No credentials. All queries to public APIs (search.nixos.org, nixhub.io, flakehub.com, noogle.dev, wiki.nixos.org). No write operations. No local filesystem access.

notes: Community listing for utensils/mcp-nixos (593 stars). Read-only NixOS ecosystem reference. 2 tools, 10 search sources. Zero auth. Implements documentation-v1 (required + recommended). Pairs with virtualisation-v1 providers (vultr-blade-mcp, runpod-mcp) for declarative infrastructure workflows.
